contentBlocks.original:
शरीरं सुरूपं तथा वा कलत्रं ।
यशश्चारु चित्रं धनं मेरुतुल्यम् ।
मनश्चेन्न लग्नं गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे ।
ततः किं ततः किं ततः किं ततः किम् ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śarīraṁ surūpaṁ tathā vā kalatraṁ |
yaśaścāru citraṁ dhanaṁ mērutulyam |
manaścēnna lagnaṁ gurōraṅghripadmē |
tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kim || 1 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Even if one possesses a beautiful body, a lovely spouse, wonderful fame, and wealth equal to Mount Meru (immense wealth) - if one's mind is not devoted to the lotus feet of the Guru (spiritual teacher), then what is the use? What is the use? What is the use? What is the use?
contentBlocks.meanings: शरीरं (śarīraṁ) – body; सुरूपं (surūpaṁ) – beautiful; तथा (tathā) – also; वा (vā) – or; कलत्रं (kalatraṁ) – spouse; यशः (yaśaḥ) – fame; चारु (cāru) – lovely; चित्रं (citraṁ) – wonderful; धनं (dhanaṁ) – wealth; मेरुतुल्यम् (merutulyam) – equal to Mount Meru (meru:Mount Meru | tulyam:equal to); मनः (manaḥ) – mind; चेत् (cet) – if; न (na) – not; लग्नं (lagnaṁ) – devoted; गुरोः (guroḥ) – of the Guru; अङ्घ्रिपद्मे (aṅghripadme) – to the lotus feet (aṅghri:feet | padme:lotus); ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what is the use; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what is the use; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what is the use; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किम् (kim) – what is the use;

contentBlocks.original:
कलत्रं धनं पुत्रपौत्रादि सर्वं ।
गृहं बान्धवाः सर्वमेतद्धि जातम् ।
मनश्चेन्न लग्नं गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे ।
ततः किं ततः किं ततः किं ततः किम् ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalatraṁ dhanaṁ putrapautrādi sarvaṁ |
gr̥haṁ bāndhavāḥ sarvamētaddhi jātam |
manaścēnna lagnaṁ gurōraṅghripadmē |
tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kim || 2 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Even if one has a spouse, wealth, sons, grandsons and everything else, a home, and relatives - even if all these have been attained - if one's mind is not devoted to the lotus feet of the Guru, then what is the use? What is the use? What is the use? What is the use?
contentBlocks.meanings: कलत्रं (kalatraṁ) – spouse; धनं (dhanaṁ) – wealth; पुत्रपौत्रादि (putrapautrādi) – sons and grandsons etc. (putra:sons | pautra:grandsons | ādi:etc.); सर्वं (sarvaṁ) – everything; गृहं (gṛhaṁ) – home; बान्धवाः (bāndhavāḥ) – relatives; सर्वमेतद्धि (sarvam etad dhi) – all this indeed (sarvam:all | etat:this | hi:indeed); जातम् (jātam) – attained; मनश्चेन्न (manaś cen na) – if mind not (manaḥ:mind | cet:if | na:not); लग्नं (lagnaṁ) – devoted; गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे (guror aṅghripadme) – to Guru's lotus feet (guroḥ:of Guru | aṅghri:feet | padme:lotus); ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what is the use; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what is the use; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what is the use; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किम् (kim) – what is the use;

contentBlocks.original:
षडङ्गादिवेदो मुखे शास्त्रविद्या ।
कवित्वादि गद्यं सुपद्यं करोति ।
मनश्चेन्न लग्नं गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे ।
ततः किं ततः किं ततः किं ततः किम् ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṣaḍaṅgādivēdō mukhē śāstravidyā |
kavitvādi gadyaṁ supadyaṁ karōti |
manaścēnna lagnaṁ gurōraṅghripadmē |
tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kim || 3 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Even if one masters the Vedas with their six branches, has all scriptural knowledge on their tongue, and can compose beautiful prose and poetry - if one's mind is not devoted to the lotus feet of the Guru, then what is the use? What is the use? What is the use? What is the use?
contentBlocks.meanings: षडङ्गादिवेदः (ṣaḍaṅgādivedaḥ) – Vedas with six branches (ṣaṭ:six | aṅga:limbs/branches | ādi:and others | vedaḥ:Vedas); मुखे (mukhe) – in the mouth; शास्त्रविद्या (śāstravidyā) – scriptural knowledge (śāstra:scripture | vidyā:knowledge); कवित्वादि (kavitvādi) – poetry and others (kavitva:poetry | ādi:and others); गद्यम् (gadyam) – prose; सुपद्यम् (supadyam) – beautiful poetry; करोति (karoti) – does; मनः (manaḥ) – mind; चेत् (cet) – if; न (na) – not; लग्नम् (lagnam) – attached; गुरोः (guroḥ) – of the Guru; अङ्घ्रिपद्मे (aṅghripadme) – lotus feet (aṅghri:feet | padme:lotus); ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किम् (kim) – what;

contentBlocks.original:
विदेशेषु मान्यः स्वदेशेषु धन्यः ।
सदाचारवृत्तेषु मत्तो न चान्यः ।
मनश्चेन्न लग्नं गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे ।
ततः किं ततः किं ततः किं ततः किम् ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidēśēṣu mānyaḥ svadēśēṣu dhanyaḥ |
sadācāravr̥ttēṣu mattō na cānyaḥ |
manaścēnna lagnaṁ gurōraṅghripadmē |
tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kim || 4 |
contentBlocks.translation: “I am honoured in other lands and I am prosperous in my homeland; in the paths of righteous conduct there is none who surpasses me”, thus one may think; yet - if one's mind is not devoted to the lotus feet of the Guru, then what is the use? What is the use? What is the use? What is the use?
contentBlocks.meanings: विदेशेषु (videśeṣu) – in foreign lands (vid:foreign | deśeṣu:in lands); मान्यः (mānyaḥ) – honoured; स्वदेशेषु (svadeśeṣu) – in one's own land (sva:own | deśeṣu:in lands); धन्यः (dhanyaḥ) – prosperous; सदाचारवृत्तेषु (sadācāravṛtteṣu) – in righteous conduct (sad:good | ācāra:conduct | vṛtteṣu:in paths); मत्तः (mattaḥ) – than me; न (na) – not; च (ca) – and; अन्यः (anyaḥ) – another; मनः (manaḥ) – mind; चेत् (cet) – if; न (na) – not; लग्नं (lagnaṁ) – attached; गुरोः (guroḥ) – of the Guru; अङ्घ्रिपद्मे (aṅghripadme) – to the lotus feet (aṅghri:feet | padme:lotus); ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किम् (kim) – what;

contentBlocks.original:
क्षमामण्डले भूपभूपालबृन्दैः ।
सदा सेवितं यस्य पादारविन्दम् ।
मनश्चेन्न लग्नं गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे ।
ततः किं ततः किं ततः किं ततः किम् ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣamāmaṇḍalē bhūpabhūpālabr̥ndaiḥ |
sadā sēvitaṁ yasya pādāravindam |
manaścēnna lagnaṁ gurōraṅghripadmē |
tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kim || 5 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Even if one's feet are constantly served/attended to by crowds of kings and emperors across the entire earth - if one's mind is not devoted to the lotus feet of the Guru (gurōraṅghripadmē), then what is the use? What is the use? What is the use? What is the use?
contentBlocks.meanings: क्षमामण्डले (kṣamāmaṇḍale) – on the earth (kṣamā:earth | maṇḍale:on the circle); भूपभूपालबृन्दैः (bhūpabhūpālabr̥ndaiḥ) – by groups of kings (bhūpa:king | bhūpāla:protector of earth | br̥ndaiḥ:by groups); सदा (sadā) – always; सेवितं (sevitaṁ) – served; यस्य (yasya) – whose; पादारविन्दम् (pādāravindam) – lotus feet (pāda:feet | aravindam:lotus); मनश्चेत् (manaścet) – if the mind (manaḥ:mind | cet:if); न (na) – not; लग्नं (lagnaṁ) – attached; गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे (guroraṅghripadme) – to the Guru's lotus feet (guroḥ:of the Guru | aṅghri:feet | padme:lotus); ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किम् (kim) – what;

contentBlocks.original:
यशो मे गतं दिक्षु दानप्रतापा-
ज्जगद्वस्तु सर्वं करे यत्प्रसादात् ।
मनश्चेन्न लग्नं गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे ।
ततः किं ततः किं ततः किं ततः किम् ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaśō mē gataṁ dikṣu dānapratāpā-
jjagadvastu sarvaṁ karē yatprasādāt |
manaścēnna lagnaṁ gurōraṅghripadmē |
tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kim || 6 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Even if one's fame has spread in all directions through charitable acts and prowess, and by whose grace all the world's things are in one's hand/control - if one's mind is not devoted to the lotus feet of the Guru, then what is the use? What is the use? What is the use? What is the use?
contentBlocks.meanings: यशः (yaśaḥ) – fame; मे (me) – my; गतं (gataṁ) – gone; दिक्षु (dikṣu) – in all directions; दानप्रतापात् (dānapratāpāt) – by charity and prowess (dāna:charity | pratāpāt:by prowess); जगद्वस्तु (jagadvastu) – all world's things (jagat:world | vastu:things); सर्वं (sarvaṁ) – all; करे (kare) – in hand; यत् (yat) – by whose; प्रसादात् (prasādāt) – by grace; मनः (manaḥ) – mind; चेत् (cet) – if; न (na) – not; लग्नं (lagnaṁ) – attached; गुरोः (guroḥ) – of the Guru; अङ्घ्रिपद्मे (aṅghripadme) – to the lotus feet (aṅghri:feet | padme:lotus); ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किम् (kim) – what;

contentBlocks.original:
न भोगे न योगे न वा वाजिराजौ ।
न कान्तामुखे नैव वित्तेषु चित्तम् ।
मनश्चेन्न लग्नं गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे ।
ततः किं ततः किं ततः किं ततः किम् ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na bhōgē na yōgē na vā vājirājau |
na kāntāmukhē naiva vittēṣu cittam |
manaścēnna lagnaṁ gurōraṅghripadmē |
tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kim || 7 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Even if one's mind is not attached to pleasures, nor to yoga, nor to the finest horses, nor to the face of the beloved, nor even to wealth - if one's mind is still not devoted to the lotus feet of the Guru, then what is the use? What is the use? What is the use? What is the use?
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; भोगे (bhoge) – in pleasures; न (na) – not; योगे (yoge) – in yoga; न (na) – not; वा (vā) – or; वाजिराजौ (vājirājau) – in the finest horses (vāji:horse | rājau:finest); न (na) – not; कान्तामुखे (kāntāmukhe) – in the face of beloved (kāntā:beloved | mukhe:in the face); नैव (naiva) – not even; वित्तेषु (vitteṣu) – in wealth; चित्तम् (cittam) – mind; मनश्चेत् (manaścet) – if the mind (manaḥ:mind | cet:if); न (na) – not; लग्नं (lagnaṁ) – attached; गुरोः (guroḥ) – of the Guru; अङ्घ्रिपद्मे (aṅghripadme) – to the lotus feet (aṅghri:feet | padme:lotus); ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṁ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किम् (kim) – what;

contentBlocks.original:
अरण्ये न वा स्वस्य गेहे न कार्ये ।
न देहे मनो वर्तते मे त्वनर्घ्ये ।
मनश्चेन्न लग्नं गुरोरङ्घ्रिपद्मे ।
ततः किं ततः किं ततः किं ततः किम् ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
araṇyē na vā svasya gēhē na kāryē |
na dēhē manō vartatē mē tvanarghyē |
manaścēnna lagnaṁ gurōraṅghripadmē |
tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kiṁ tataḥ kim || 8 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Even if one's mind does not dwell in the forest (solitude), nor in one's home, nor in duties, nor in this priceless body - if one's mind is still not devoted to the lotus feet of the Guru, then what is the use? What is the use? What is the use? What is the use?
contentBlocks.meanings: अरण्ये (araṇye) – in the forest; न (na) – not; वा (vā) – or; स्वस्य (svasya) – one's own; गेहे (gehe) – in the home; न (na) – not; कार्ये (kārye) – in duties; न (na) – not; देहे (dehe) – in the body; मनो (mano) – mind; वर्तते (vartate) – dwells; मे (me) – my; त्वनर्घ्ये (tvanarghye) – in the priceless (tvam:your | anarghye:priceless); मनश्चेत् (manaścet) – if the mind (manaḥ:mind | cet:if); न (na) – not; लग्नं (lagnaṃ) – attached; गुरोः (guroḥ) – of the Guru; अङ्घ्रिपद्मे (aṅghripadme) – to the lotus feet (aṅghri:feet | padme:lotus); ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṃ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṃ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किं (kiṃ) – what; ततः (tataḥ) – then; किम् (kim) – what;

contentBlocks.original:
गुरोरष्टकं यः पठेत्पुण्यदेही ।
यतिर्भूपतिर्ब्रह्मचारी च गेही ।
लभेद्वाञ्छितार्थं पदं ब्रह्मसञ्ज्ञं ।
गुरोरुक्तवाक्ये मनो यस्य लग्नं ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gurōraṣṭakaṁ yaḥ paṭhētpuṇyadēhī |
yatirbhūpatirbrahmacārī ca gēhī |
labhēdvāñchitārthaṁ padaṁ brahmasañjñaṁ |
gurōruktavākyē manō yasya lagnaṁ ||
contentBlocks.translation: One who recites these eight verses in praise of the Guru - be they a renunciate, a king, a spiritual student, or a householder - will attain their desired goals and the supreme state of Brahman (enlightenment), if their mind remains firmly fixed on the Guru's teachings.
contentBlocks.meanings: गुरोः (guroḥ) – of the Guru; अष्टकम् (aṣṭakam) – eight verses; यः (yaḥ) – who; पठेत् (paṭhet) – recites; पुण्यदेही (puṇyadehī) – virtuous person (puṇya:virtuous | dehī:person); यतिः (yatiḥ) – renunciate; भूपतिः (bhūpatiḥ) – king; ब्रह्मचारी (brahmacārī) – spiritual student; च (ca) – and; गेही (gehī) – householder; लभेत् (labhet) – will attain; वाञ्छितार्थम् (vāñchitārtham) – desired goals (vāñchita:desired | artham:goals); पदम् (padam) – state; ब्रह्मसञ्ज्ञम् (brahmasañjñam) – supreme state of Brahman (brahma:Brahman | sañjñam:state); गुरोः (guroḥ) – of the Guru; उक्तवाक्ये (uktavākye) – in the spoken words (ukta:spoken | vākye:words); मनः (manaḥ) – mind; यस्य (yasya) – whose; लग्नम् (lagnam) – fixed;
